search_queries_used: site:bsaci.org rhinitis guideline nasal polyps history examination
site:entnet.org adult sinusitis update 2025 polyps modifying factors objective confirmation
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic steroid, biologic, CT, endoscopy, surgery, biopsy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Symptoms and anterior examination do not establish polyp identity, laterality, extent, or cause.
No investigation, medicine, treatment, procedure, biopsy, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with clinician-suspected nasal polyps; children, unilateral mass, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease, and tumour concern require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, allergy, respiratory, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE allergy, respiratory, imaging, endoscopy, biologic, pathology, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BSACI 2017 and AAO-HNSF 2025 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The AAO-HNSF 2025 update is current; BSACI 2017 remains listed with recency limitations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact rhinitis history section supports onset, obstruction, smell, discharge, sleep impact, asthma or allergy context, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without confirming a nasal polyp.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed nasal and throat examination including polyp observation when assessed; it does not infer histology or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact chronic-rhinosinusitis section supports documenting presence or absence of polyps, modifying factors, and existing imaging or specialist reports without ordering CT, endoscopy, or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, laterality, smell, obstruction, bleeding, asthma, aspirin sensitivity, examination, endoscopy, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, pathology, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
